instructions: Review recipient evidence, draft angle, CTA, landing-page handoff, objection plan, and review flags before setting a decision.
Set decision to approve, edit, or reject only for rows the founder/operator has actually reviewed.
Do not approve pricing, legal, privacy, permission, rights, readiness, live spend, sender durability, or live dispatch from this packet.

Live sends remain blocked until decisions are applied, npm run gtm:send -- --dry-run --allow-blocked is rerun, npm run human-replies:audit-durability passes, and live dispatch is explicitly authorized.
Leave decision null for rows that have not been reviewed.
reviewChecklist: Recipient email and evidence source are acceptable for a first touch.
Message asks one clear thing and stays aligned to the row track.
Proof source matches the CTA and does not overstate what exists.
Proof-ready rows link to a labeled review/sample without claiming customer traction.
Demand-sourced rows ask for the site/workflow to capture and do not imply a hosted review exists.
Blocked claims are preserved in the final draft and approval note.
Objection response can be answered from ledger evidence and public pages without inventing claims.
decisionOptions: approve: row may move to human_approved after apply, but live send remains separately gated.
edit: row stays draft_ready with a required revision note.
reject: row stays blocked until the target, proof artifact, recipient, or message is revised.

approvals.recipientEvidenceSource: Fetched Simbe Robotics contact page lists SALES & DEPLOYMENT as sales@simberobotics.com.
approvals.workflowNeed: Inspect a grocery-style public aisle loop before deciding whether a more specific retail capture would be useful.
intentSignals: Company publicly sells retail shelf-intelligence robots that operate in grocery and store aisles.
approvals.proofSource: Labeled hosted-review proof at client/public/samples/sample-hosted-review-report.md. Hosted review handoff: /sample-evaluation. Site-world id: sample-public-capture-cedar-market-aisle-loop. Use as representative proof shape only; do not claim recipient/customer-specific proof.
approvals.draftAngle: Invite the recipient to inspect a labeled exact-site hosted review, then ask what site or workflow would make the review more relevant.
approvals.cta: Inspect the review, then name the more relevant site or workflow.
approvals.objectionPlan: If they say the sample is not their site, ask for the exact workflow to capture next and keep the sample labeled as representative proof shape.
blockedClaims: No live send, reply, hosted-review start, qualified call, customer traction, paid spend, sender durability, or dispatch authorization is approved by this packet.
No pricing, legal, privacy, rights, permission, readiness, deployment, or guaranteed support commitment is approved by this packet.
Do not present the sample review as the recipient's site, a customer result, or a deployment outcome.
Recipient-backed evidence proves the address source only; it does not prove buyer intent or permission to make unsupported claims.
reviewFlags: public/general inbox; expect routing friction
